title: 10 best herbal capsules for runny nose

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with runny nose because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The capsules also contain antioxidants that combat free radicals, which can contribute to chronic sinusitis and upper respiratory infections. Additionally, the herb has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from a runny nose and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: The bioactive compounds in Kansas Coneflower, such as cichoric acid and caffeic acid, have been shown to inhibit histamine release, which can help reduce nasal congestion and runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower contains a variety of flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce swelling and excess mucus production in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.

Immune system modulation: The saponins and phenolic acids present in Kansas Coneflower have been found to modulate the immune response, increasing the production of cytokines that help to fight off infections and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms, including a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose.

Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia, such as echinamine and isobutylamides, possess immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune system's response to allergens and reduce symptoms like runny nose.

Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates, particularly the beta-glucans, have immunostimulatory properties that enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms, helping to combat infections and reduce symptoms associated with runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are considered the most potent part of Echinacea angustifolia, containing high concentrations of active compounds.
Rhyzomes: They are often used as a substitute for roots, as they have similar medicinal properties and can be easier to harvest.
Seeds: They are sometimes used to make capsules due to their potential anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and stems containing 4 to 8 percent alkylamides.
Grind the dried echinacea angustifolia material into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix the ground echinacea powder with 50 grams of a natural filler such as cellulose or starch.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the echinacea powder mixture.
Store the herbal echinacea capsules in a cool dark place for up to one year after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with runny nose because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that calm and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages. These capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a reduced flow of mucus. Additionally, maidenhair tree has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that help to combat infections that can cause runny nose, providing long-lasting relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Ginkgo biloba capsules have antihistamine effects, which can help to reduce histamine release in the body and alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The extracts of Maidenhair Tree capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating a runny nose and sinus pressure.

Expectorant properties: Ginkgo biloba's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and inflammation associated with runny nose.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a sesquiterpene lactone, has been shown to have antihistamine-like properties, which can help alleviate runny nose symptoms by blocking histamine receptors and reducing allergic responses.

Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides, a type of sesquiterpene lactone, have been found to inhibit platelet-activating factor (PAF), a chemical mediator involved in allergic reactions, which may help alleviate runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help relieve congestion and nasal discharge.
Seeds: They contain ginkgolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce nasal inflammation and relieve runny nose symptoms.
Barks: They contain proanthocyanidins, which may help to reduce nasal congestion and improve respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of ginkgo biloba dried leaves and flowers and 100 grams of gelatin capsules.
Mill the ginkgo biloba dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mill for 30 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50 grams of starch and 10 grams of silica to enhance flow properties.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture using an encapsulation machine and 30 minutes to fill 100 capsules.
Label and package the ginkgo biloba capsules in airtight containers to preserve their potency for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with runny nose because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry, which soothes and calms irritated nasal passages. The anthocyanins in elderberry have been shown to reduce swelling and ease congestion, providing fast and effective relief from sinus pressure and runny noses. Additionally, elderberry's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that can cause excess mucus production, allowing the body to naturally heal and recover.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Elderberry's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help reduce nasal inflammation and congestion, alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.

Decongestant and Anti-Histamine Effects: The bioactive compounds in elderberry capsules, like quercetin, possess decongestant and anti-histamine properties that can help shrink blood vessels, reducing nasal swelling and excess mucus production.

Immune System Support: Elderberry's immunomodulatory effects can help stimulate the immune system to produce cytokines, which in turn, support the body's natural defense against viral and bacterial infections that may contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Isovitexin: A flavonoid glycoside that helps reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.
Dehydrovomifoliol: A lignan glycoside with antiviral properties that helps combat the underlying viral infections causing runny nose, such as the common cold and flu.

Vitamin C (Ascorbic acid): An antioxidant that helps boost the immune system, reducing the severity of cold and flu symptoms, including runny nose, by promoting the production of white blood cells and other immune cells.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain flavonoids and alkaloids that help reduce nasal discharge and alleviate congestion.
Leaves: They have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in treating respiratory issues and reducing nasal secretions.
Fruits: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe the nasal passages and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the early morning when they are still dewy and fragrant.
Dry the harvested plant material in a temperature-controlled environment at 40°c for 24 to 48 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of excipient to create a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the blend into vegetarian capsules using a capsule filling machine or by hand with a gelatin capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia capsules helps with runny nose because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The herb stevia, also known as sweet leaf, has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as congestion and sinusitis. When taken in capsule form, it can help to dry up excess mucus and alleviate discomfort associated with a runny nose. Additionally, stevia capsules may also help to boost the immune system, further supporting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Stevia's antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and sinus pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and other bioactive compounds present in stevia capsules have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which may help to reduce swelling and mucus production in the nasal tissues, leading to a runny nose.
Expectorant properties: Stevia's saponins can help thin and loosen mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel, thereby providing relief from a runny nose and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Steviol Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Catechins: As a type of phenolic compound, catechins have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate nasal congestion and excess mucus production.

Flavonoids: Specifically, the flavonoids present in Stevia rebaudiana, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of Stevia rebaudiana's medicinal properties, which help alleviate symptoms of a runny nose due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Stevia rebaudiana roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making them useful in capsule form for runny nose relief.
Flowers: Stevia rebaudiana flowers possess antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which may help combat the underlying causes of a runny nose, such as viral infections or allergies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of stevia rebaudiana leaves and stems with a sharp knife or pruning tool.
Dry the harvested material in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Weigh 25 grams of the dried stevia rebaudiana and blend it into a fine powder.
Mix 5 grams of the powder with 5 grams of tapioca starch and 5 grams of gelatin to create a capsule filling.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the stevia rebaudiana mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with runny nose because they contain a concentrated extract of thymol, a natural antiseptic compound that has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues. The capsules work by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which helps to alleviate congestion and runny nose caused by colds, allergies, and other conditions. Additionally, thyme's antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and relieving symptoms of a runny nose.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol, help reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to a runny nose and sinus pressure.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause a runny nose, including colds, flu, and allergies, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties that help combat the underlying infection causing the runny nose, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and alleviating runny nose symptoms.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate has expectorant properties that help thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the nasal passages and reducing the feeling of congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in thymol and other compounds, thyme leaves are primarily used to create capsules for their decongestant and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain a higher concentration of thymol and other volatile oils, which contribute to their effectiveness in relieving congestion and nasal discharge.

Buds: Thyme buds are often harvested before the flowers bloom and contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, making them a popular choice for capsules aimed at addressing respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried thymus vulgaris flowers and leaves from organic sources by mid-morning.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours slowly.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 3 minutes speed.
Mix 250mg of the powdered material with 250mg of vegetable cellulose and 25mg of silicon dioxide.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo capsules helps with runny nose because of its unique ability to combat inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. The natural ingredients, including polyphenols and saponins, work together to reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure, allowing for a smoother flow of mucus and a significant decrease in runniness. Additionally, umckaloabo's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the nasal tissues, providing long-lasting relief from chronic congestion and sinus issues.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Umckaloabo capsules have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, reducing the severity and duration of respiratory infections, including those that cause runny noses.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules contain compounds that reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can help alleviate congestion and discharge associated with a runny nose.
Mucolytic properties: Umckaloabo's mucolytic agents break down excess mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the thickness and stickiness of nasal secretions, allowing for clearer breathing.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: Ursolic acid, a triterpenoid, helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating congestion and runny nose.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic acid, acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to combat the underlying causes of runny nose, such as viral infections and allergies.
Geraniol: Geraniol, a terpene, has decongestant properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their high content of bioactive compounds, which are believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
Leaves: They are used for their similar bioactive compound content to the roots, providing similar benefits for runny nose relief.
Stems: They are also used for their bioactive compounds, although to a lesser extent compared to roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of pelargonium sidoides roots and leaves from a trusted supplier or cultivated source.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 250 grams of the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of starch and 50 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100-count gelatin capsules with 400 milligrams of the blend each and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with runny nose because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce congestion and swelling in the nasal passages. These acids also exhibit mucolytic activity, breaking down excess mucus and making it easier to expel. As a result, frankincense capsules may provide relief from constant runny nose, sinus pressure, and postnasal drip, helping to promote clear breathing and overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense capsules reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which helps to alleviate congestion and runny nose by reducing swelling and irritation.

Antihistamine properties: Frankincense contains natural antihistamines that can help to block the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions and subsequent runny nose, sneezing, and itching.

Antimicrobial properties: Frankincense capsules have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and viruses, which can help to combat infections that may contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): This triterpenoid helps reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.
Boswellic acids: These triterpenoid compounds, including AKBA, have anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties, which help to alleviate nasal congestion, runny nose, and other respiratory issues.
α-pinene: This terpene has natural decongestant properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion and runny nose by thinning mucus and promoting its clearance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of runny nose and other respiratory issues.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are also a rich source of boswellic acids, contributing to their use in capsules for runny nose due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin from a trusted supplier or by drying fresh resin.
Dry the harvested boswellia serrata resin at 50°c for 24 hours in a food dehydrator.
Weigh 250 grams of the dried boswellia serrata resin and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground boswellia serrata powder with 250 grams of gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill the gelatin capsules with the boswellia serrata powder mixture using a capsule filling machine within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden tulip capsules helps with runny nose because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the nasal passages. The extracts from tulips, such as tulsi and turmeric, work to reduce congestion and inflammation, allowing the body to breathe easier and promoting a healthy respiratory system. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Tulip capsules contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds present in tulip capsules can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, further reducing the severity of nasal congestion and runniness.

Expectorant property: Tulip capsules contain natural expectorants, such as mucilage, which can help loosen and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in the tulip plant have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and promote the clearance of mucus from the nasal passages.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in the tulip plant have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, thereby relieving runny nose symptoms.

Anthocyanins: Anthocyanins in the tulip plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, thereby relieving runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Tulipa gesneriana are used to make capsules that help alleviate symptoms of a runny nose due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Tulipa gesneriana are used to make herbal remedies that help combat respiratory infections, including a runny nose, due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean 100g of fresh tulipa gesneriana flowers and leaves to be used for drying.
Dry the gathered material in a dehydrator at 50c for 12 hours or air dry for 5 days.
Grind the dried 50g of tulipa gesneriana flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered 20g of tulipa gesneriana with 10g of gelatin and 10ml of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately for 24 hours storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with runny nose because they contain a concentrated form of the calming and soothing essential oil, linalool. This natural compound has been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, thus alleviating congestion and excessive mucus production. Additionally, the gentle antihistamine properties of lavender oil help to calm and soothe irritated tissues, providing quick relief from constant sniffles and sniffling. By promoting a healthy respiratory system, English lavender capsules provide a natural and effective solution for a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help reduce nasal swelling and congestion, allowing for easier breathing.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants can help protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by pollutants, allergens, and irritants, which can exacerbate a runny nose.
Sedative and relaxing properties: The sedative compounds in lavender, such as linalool, can help calm the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with runny nose by reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene acts as an antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to reduce the production of mucus and alleviate the discomfort associated with a runny nose.

Kaempferol: This flavonoid phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of essential oils, which provide antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their calming and soothing effects, as they contain lavender oil that can help reduce congestion and promote relaxation.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Dry the dried flowers of lavandula angustifolia in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or plant-based cellulose in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 1 gram of the herbal powder using a capsule filling machine.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place to preserve the potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with runny nose because they contain Eucalyptus globulus, a natural decongestant that effectively relieves nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The capsules' active ingredient, eucalyptol, is released into the body where it breaks down excess mucus, reducing its viscosity and promoting drainage. This helps to clear the airways of irritants and allergens, alleviating runny nose symptoms and leaving you breathing easier.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: The capsules contain eucalyptol, which is known to block histamine receptors, reducing nasal congestion and runny nose by minimizing the effects of allergens.
Decongestant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum Capsules contain menthol and camphor, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving stuffiness and promoting drainage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' active compounds, such as cineole and a-terpineol, have anti-inflammatory effects that help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol helps with runny nose by acting as a natural expectorant, helping to break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Catechins (specifically, epicatechin and epigallocatechin): Catechins have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating congestion and runny nose.
α-Pinene: α-Pinene is a bronchodilator that helps relax the airway muscles, making it easier to breathe and reducing the production of excess mucus that can cause runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in oils, particularly eucalyptol, which is a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory that helps relieve runny nose symptoms.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus contains salicylic acid, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the nasal passages.
Buds: Eucalyptus globulus buds are used to extract essential oils, including eucalyptol, which has decongestant and expectorant properties that can help relieve congestion and runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus by cutting them at the base of the stem when mature and fragrant.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator at 40°c for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground powder with a suitable capsule filler material in a ratio of 1:1 and fill capsules.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place and consume within 2 years of manufacturing date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with runny nose because it contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called calendula, which effectively reduces swelling in the nasal passages. This leads to a decrease in excess mucus production and congestion, providing relief from a runny nose. Additionally, calendula's antiviral properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose. By soothing and calming the nasal membranes, pot marigold capsules provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The capsules contain flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Pot Marigold capsules, including vitamins C and E, help protect the mucous membranes in the nose from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy nasal environment.

Mucilage production stimulation: The capsules contain mucilages, such as calyxic acid, which can stimulate the production of mucus in the nasal passages, helping to trap and remove irritants and allergens, and reducing excessive mucus secretion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain carotenoids and other bioactive compounds that may help to dry up excess mucus and alleviate runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when they are fully open and free of dew.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12-14 hours or by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels.
Crush the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking care not to generate excessive heat.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powdered calendula with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of filtered water to create a uniform paste, allowing it to sit for 10-15 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the paste and seal immediately, resulting in 20-25 capsules that can be stored in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with runny nose because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, it soothes and calms irritated nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and excessive mucus production. This helps to alleviate symptoms of a runny nose, including constant sniffles and postnasal drip. Additionally, licorice capsules have antiviral and antibacterial properties that help fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root's main active compound, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in licorice root helps to calm and soothe the mucous membranes in the nose, reducing inflammation and excess mucus production that can cause a runny nose.

Amino acids: Licorice root contains various amino acids, particularly glycine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making breathing easier.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, relieving symptoms of a runny nose.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues, reducing excess mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 30 grams of potato starch in a clean glass bowl for 5 minutes until well combined.
Fill empty #0 gelatin capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal tightly using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months.
Dispense 2-3 capsules orally twice daily as needed to help alleviate runny nose symptoms with a glass of water or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with runny nose because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The diuretic properties of dandelion also aid in draining excess mucus from the sinuses, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, the antioxidants present in dandelion capsules can help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, further contributing to its benefits for a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can contribute to a runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in dandelion capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.

Mucilaginous properties: Dandelion capsules contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and congestion associated with a runny nose.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help soothe and calm irritated nasal passages, reducing the frequency of runny nose episodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers for drying.
Dry harvested taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers using a food dehydrator or sun drying method.
Grind dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix ground taraxacum officinale with filler material such as silica gel or potato starch in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture using an automatic capsule filling machine or spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules help with runny nose because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the nasal passages. When the body produces excess mucus to combat infection or allergies, turmeric's potent bioactive compounds work to thin and clear it out, relieving congestion and discomfort. Regular supplementation can also strengthen the immune system, helping to prevent future episodes of runny nose and sinusitis. By targeting underlying inflammation, turmeric capsules provide effective relief from a pesky runny nose.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and sinus pressure associated with a runny nose.
Catechins: Antioxidant compounds that combat free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the nasal tissues, which can help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, thereby reducing runniness.
Fiber: Soluble fiber in turmeric capsules helps to thicken mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the amount of mucus produced, thus alleviating a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help alleviate congestion and nasal discharge.
Roots: Contains bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and diterpenes that exhibit decongestant and bronchodilator effects, making them effective for runny nose relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric rhizomes and clean them thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Grind the cleaned turmeric rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the powdered turmeric with 250mg of pure vegetable capsule material and fill the capsules using a capsule filling machine within 1 hour.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 2 years.
Take 2-3 capsules orally twice a day with warm water after meals for 6 weeks to alleviate runny nose symptoms naturally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with runny nose because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The active compounds present in the capsules, such as cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. This, in turn, helps to reduce congestion and sinus pressure, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer nose. Additionally, the warming properties of Ceylon cinnamon can also help to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel and providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon capsules contain compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which have antibacterial effects that can help combat infections in the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and runniness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamic acid, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal mucosa, leading to decreased runniness and congestion.

Decongestant and expectorant properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon capsules may help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing excess mucus production and promoting easier drainage, which can alleviate a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing nasal congestion and relieving runny nose symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Measure precisely 500 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a clean glass container for processing.
Grind the dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix 250 milliliters of boiling distilled water with the powdered cinnamomum verum to create an infusion for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool at room temperature.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the cooled cinnamomum verum infusion per capsule, sealing them immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats capsules helps with runny nose because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called beta-glucan, which soothes and calms irritated nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried oat straw avena sativa flowers and leaves for this recipe.
Grind the dried oat straw mixture into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 30 grams of the powdered oat straw with 20 grams of glycerin in a small bowl to create a soothing blend for 10 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the oat straw-glycerin mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule machine or by hand for 5 minutes per capsule.
Store the herbal avena sativa capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate them for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with runny nose because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 4 grams of the ground herb with 96 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Add water to the mixture and stir until a thick paste forms within 15 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal paste and seal them tightly for storage purposes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with runny nose because they contain natural oils that effectively soothe and calm the nasal passages. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil in capsules helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and relieve a runny nose.

remedy_recipe: Dry 20g of fresh peppermint leaves in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 2 hours.
Grind 10g of dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of powdered peppermint with 250mg of cornstarch and 50mg of magnesium stearate in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill 60-capsule capsules with the herbal mixture and seal tightly for 1 minute.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Treetop hydrangea capsules helps with runny nose because they contain powerful natural ingredients that work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. Mucolytic activity: Hydrangea root extract has been shown to have mucolytic activity, meaning it can break down mucus and make it easier to expel, which could help relieve congestion and sinus pressure associated with a runny nose.

remedy_parts: Roots: Contains bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from runny nose symptoms.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the nasal mucosa, reducing runny nose episodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hydrangea arborescens leaves in sufficient quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or oven drying at low temperatures.
Grind the dried hydrangea arborescens into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 1 tablespoon of ground hydrangea arborescens with 1/2 teaspoon of vegetable glycerin and 1/2 cup of distilled water.
Encapsulate the mixture in size zero hard gelatin capsules for a total daily dose of three capsules, taken thrice daily.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for runny nose are a natural supplement designed to alleviate excessive mucus discharge and congestion associated with colds, allergies, and sinusitis. They help by boosting the immune system, reducing inflammation, and loosening mucus. The best herbal capsules for runny nose include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea angustifolia, Pelargonium sidoides, Ephedra sinica, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Sambucus nigra, and Thymus serpyllum. If left untreated, a runny nose can lead to sleepless nights, decreased productivity, and a reduced quality of life, ultimately affecting daily routines and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal capsules are sinusitis, bronchitis, and pharyngitis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like goldenseal, echinacea, and marshmallow root help alleviate congestion, inflammation, and respiratory issues by reducing mucus production, soothing the throat, and opening up airways. They also provide antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with runny nose is Echinacea, Ginger, and Turmeric. Echinacea boosts the immune system, reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Ginger has natural decongestant properties that help clear mucus and reduce congestion. Turmeric, with its powerful anti-inflammatory properties, helps to soothe and calm the nasal passages. Together, these herbs work synergistically to alleviate runny nose symptoms, promoting a healthy and balanced respiratory system. This combination is available in various herbal capsule forms and can be easily incorporated into your daily supplement routine.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Frontiers in pharmacology, herbal capsules containing extracts of medicinal plants such as Siraitia grosvenorii, Lonicera japonica, Platycodon grandiflorus, and Glycyrrhiza uralensis can be beneficial for individuals with a runny nose, as they can help alleviate symptoms associated with chronic pharyngitis. In the study, patients with chronic pharyngitis were randomly assigned to an experimental group or a control group, and the results showed that the experimental group, which received the herbal lozenges, had a significantly lower total score of pharyngeal symptoms and signs compared to the control group. The improvement rates of specific symptoms such as pharyngeal itching, dry throat, and congestion of pharyngeal mucosa in the experimental group were also significantly higher than those in the control group.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with runny nose because they contain natural compounds that support the immune system and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The herb's active ingredients, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which helps to fight off infections and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose. This can lead to a reduction in nasal congestion and a decrease in the frequency and severity of runny nose episodes.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline Alkaloids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators.
Triterpene Glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the body's immune response and reduce nasal discharge.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation, stabilize mast cells, and prevent excessive mucus production in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, which are believed to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Roots: The roots are rich in echinacosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties that can help alleviate a runny nose.
Leaves: They contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including caffeic acid, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2-3 hours.
Grind 1 pound of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 mg of the ground echinacea powder with 500 mg of gelatin in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty capsules with the echinacea-gelatin mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, gingerol, found in these capsules has decongestant properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate the runny nose associated with colds and allergies. Additionally, ginger has a soothing effect on the nasal passages, promoting a reduction in mucus production and helping to clear out excess fluids, providing relief from persistent runny noses. Regular consumption of these capsules can help alleviate symptoms.

Volatile oils (specifically, borneol): Borneol has a decongestant effect, helping to clear mucus from the nasal passages and relieve congestion associated with runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds that have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing runny nose symptoms.
Buds: The buds of the Zingiber officinale plant contain essential oils that have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250-500 grams of dried zingiber officinale root and 500 capsules of size 00 vegetable capsules.
Crush 50 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 50 grams of silica powder as a filler in a bowl.
Fill each capsule with 200-300 milligrams of the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 24 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with runny nose because the oil extracted from this herb contains decongestant properties that reduce nasal congestion and inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a healthy plant in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves in a warm area with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Grind 10 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5 grams of the eucalyptus globulus powder with 5 grams of vegetable capsule filler to achieve the desired consistency.
Fill 100-200 empty gelatin capsules with the eucalyptus globulus mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with runny nose because they contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to improve blood flow to the sinuses, which can help to thin out mucus and promote drainage. remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate congestion and sinus issues.
Seeds: Seeds are often used because they contain ginkgotoxin, a compound thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce nasal discharge.
Roots: Roots are sometimes used due to their purported ability to help regulate the body's response to allergens, which may contribute to a reduction in runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and 100 grams of vegetable cellulose powder.
Combine the ginkgo biloba leaves and vegetable cellulose powder in a blender and blend for 2 minutes.
Add 200 grams of glycerin and blend the mixture for another 2 minutes to create a uniform paste.
Use a capsule machine to fill 1000 empty capsules with the herbal paste, following the manufacturer's dosage guidelines.
Label the capsules with the name, dosage, and preparation date and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia capsules helps with runny nose because they contain compounds that stimulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall season when the plants are dormant.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill.
Mix the powdered root with 200g of gelatin and 100g of vegetable glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 100 capsules with the mixture and seal them with a capsule machine within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pelargonium sidoides capsules helps with runny nose because they contain an extract from the African geranium plant, which has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. The capsules are rich in compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce congestion and clear mucus from the nasal passages. remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most used parts of Pelargonium sidoides for making capsules for runny nose because they contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.

Stems: Stems are used to some extent, but the exact reason is not well-documented, although it's believed that they may contain some of the plant's medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh roots of pelargonium sidoides with a diameter of 1-2 cm each, in the morning.
Clean the harvested roots with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris, in 10 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots in a well-ventilated area at 40-50°c for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mill or mortar and pestle, in 30 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with 250-500mg of the powdered root, seal them, and store them in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica capsules helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties, which effectively reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure. remedy_parts: Stems: They are the primary source of ephedrine, a key ingredient in capsules for runny nose due to its decongestant properties.

Seeds: Ephedrine-rich seeds are sometimes used to make capsules for runny nose, although they may not be as commonly used as stems and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of ephedra sinica roots dried to 10 moisture content within 3 days.
Grind the ephedra sinica roots into a fine powder using a mill within 30 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the ephedra sinica powder with 250 grams of gelatin powder within 15 minutes.
Fill the gelatin mixture into empty capsules using a capsule filler within 30 minutes.
Store the ephedra sinica capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight indefinitely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with runny nose because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing congestion and inflammation. Licoricidin and licorisoflavan A: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and runny nose by suppressing the release of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are most commonly used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps in reducing nasal congestion.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used due to their ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of runny nose.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used due to their rich antioxidant content, which helps in reducing inflammation and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root from a trusted supplier or cultivate it in a controlled environment.
Dry the harvested root material in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 250 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 50 grams of gelatin powder and 100 milliliters of glycerin in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal compound.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra capsules helps with runny nose because they contain the active compounds found in elderberry flowers, which have natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat underlying infections and reduce nasal discharge.
Saponins: These compounds have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part for making capsules to alleviate a runny nose due to their high content of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and saponins.

Grind the dried elderberries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine 15 grams of the elderberry powder with 10 grams of gelatin in a mixing bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal sambucus nigra capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum capsules helps with runny nose because they contain thyme oil, a natural decongestant and expectorant. Thyme oil has potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and thin mucus, making it easier to expel. Stems: Thymus serpyllum stems are used in herbal remedies for runny nose because they contain compounds that help reduce congestion and promote sinus drainage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of thymus serpyllum flowers in late summer or early autumn for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind 50g of dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for uniform consistency.
Mix 10g of the powder with 10g of vegetable cellulose and 10g of silicon dioxide to create a blend.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the blend and seal them immediately to preserve freshness and potency.
